page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmgn8te.32xlarge is an x86_64 architecture bare-metal server instance powered by Intel Xeon 6462C processors. The system features 128 dedicated physical cores running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz, utilizing a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing an allocation of 8.0 GB per core.

This instance integrates eight NVIDIA Ada Lovelace architecture GPUs, delivering a combined total of 384 GB of VRAM. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 170 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers. The instance does not include local storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions for data persistence.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high resource density by combining massive parallel GPU compute capacity with dedicated CPU cores, though the lack of local storage requires careful architectural planning for storage-intensive tasks. This hardware profile is designed for GPU-accelerated workloads, including deep learning model training, large-scale AI inference, high-performance computing, and complex parallel processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmgn8te.32xlarge is a high-density, GPU-accelerated bare-metal server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 dedicated Intel Xeon 6462C physical cores operating at 3.3 GHz with a single thread per core, paired with 1024.0 GB of system memory. Acceleration is driven by eight NVIDIA Ada Lovelace GPUs providing a total of 384 GB of VRAM. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 170 Gbps. The instance does not feature local storage, meaning all data persistence must be handled via network-attached storage. Given its massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed networking, this server is optimized for intensive workloads such as artificial intelligence training, large language model inference, and high-performance computing.

tagline: A bare-metal server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors with eight NVIDIA Ada Lovelace GPUs for high-throughput parallel computing.
